overview.short: Chandragad Fort / Dhavalgad Fort is a fort located 182 km from Mumbai, in raigad district, of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Chandragad Fort / Dhavalgad Fort is a fort located 182 km from Mumbai, in raigad district, of Maharashtra. This fort was an important fort in raigad district as a watch over for the Varandha Ghattrade routes from Bhor to kokan. The fort is surrounded by forest and hill slopes.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Pratapgad
topPlaces.description: Pratapgad is a mountain fort located in Satara district, in the Western Indian state of Maharashtra. The fort is situated 24 kilometres from the Mahabaleshwar hill station.

topPlaces.name: Venna Lake
topPlaces.description: Venna Lake is one of the tourist attractions of Mahabaleshwar in Maharashtra state in India. The lake was constructed in 1842 by Shahaji, the last raja (king) of the Satara princely state.

topPlaces.name: Lok Bhavan, Mahabaleshwar
topPlaces.description: Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan, Mahabaleshwar is the summer residence of the Governor of Maharashtra. It is located in the hill station town of Mahabaleshwar, Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Mahabaleshwar
topPlaces.description: Mahabaleshwar () is a small town and a municipal council in Satara district, Maharashtra, India. It is a place of pilgrimage because the Krishna River, which considered sacred by Hindus, has its origin here along with five of its tributaries.

topPlaces.name: Old Mahabaleshwar
topPlaces.description: Old Mahabaleshwar, also known as Kshetra Mahabaleshwar, is a historical village in Mahabaleshwar, of the Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a hill station located near the Western Ghats, seven kilometers from Mahabaleshwar.

items.note: Pratapgad is a mountain fort located in Satara district, in the Western Indian state of Maharashtra. The fort is situated 24 kilometres from the Mahabaleshwar hill station.
Venna Lake is one of the tourist attractions of Mahabaleshwar in Maharashtra state in India. The lake was constructed in 1842 by Shahaji, the last raja (king) of the Satara princely state.
Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan, Mahabaleshwar is the summer residence of the Governor of Maharashtra. It is located in the hill station town of Mahabaleshwar, Maharashtra.

Mahabaleshwar () is a small town and a municipal council in Satara district, Maharashtra, India. It is a place of pilgrimage because the Krishna River, which considered sacred by Hindus, has its origin here along with five of its tributaries.

Old Mahabaleshwar, also known as Kshetra Mahabaleshwar, is a historical village in Mahabaleshwar, of the Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a hill station located near the Western Ghats, seven kilometers from Mahabaleshwar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chandragad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chandragad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (72 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (64 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chandragad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chandragad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chandragad?
faq.a: Stays in Chandragad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chandragad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pratapgad, Venna Lake, Lok Bhavan, Mahabaleshwar, Mahabaleshwar, Old Mahabaleshwar.
